svg.title: CLIPittyClip Suite v3.5 — wizard flow
svg.desc: Two entry points (top-level launcher or per-tool -w flag) feed a tool-triage dispatcher and four per-tool wizards, each ending in a final confirmation step that emits an equivalent CLI command and launches the chosen pipeline.
svg.text: Two entry points → tool triage → per-tool wizard → final confirmation → auto-launch

g.text: runs chosen wizard, eval's the equivalent CLI.
Per-tool direct entry. Skips triage and
goes straight to that tool's wizard.
"Which tool would you like to configure?"
peak calling → CTK* → Clink* → motif → grouping*
After "Start? [Y/n]" — auto-launch (Entry A) or hand-back (Entry B)
• Each wizard exports CLIPITTY_EQUIV_CMD — the full equivalent CLI command for the run.
• Entry A (CLIPittyClip_wizard.sh): prepends repo dir to PATH, then eval "$CLIPITTY_EQUIV_CMD" → real tool runs immediately.
• Entry B (<Tool>.sh -w): wizard returns to its host script, WIZ_* env vars feed the existing flag-parsing block, host runs.
• Equivalent CLI is also printed to terminal and to the run's log file — copy-paste to reproduce non-interactively next time.
• All read -p calls are EOF-guarded: closed stdin aborts with a clear message instead of spinning.

svg.text: CLIPittyClip Suite v3.5 — wizard implementation as merged on v3-wizard-update.
Categories marked * in lane 1 are track-conditional (CTK requires --run-cims/--run-cits; Clink requires --run-clink; Grouping requires multi-sample input).
